title: Name of Adams children
wikitext:
[]: Name of Adam's children             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Aug 31, 2006 9:22 pm if i remember right, the term angel was given to the children of Adam my the Lillim. Shouldnt the Children of Adam have a real name? Im not even sure what language is the source of the Lillith-Lillim relationship, so i cant even take a stab at that... I think that the term Adamite is a valid term. "-ite" means decended from, thus Adamite means the decendants of Adam. Have you ever wondered what the Adamites call us?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Aug 31, 2006 9:46 pm All the names were assigned by SEELE, so it's all kind of irrelivant.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 31, 2006 9:53 pm If memory serves, weren't the names in the dead sea scrolls, which Seele merely posesses? But then again, memory does not serve me well. But, let's not forget, religion in Eva hardly ever has any bearing or greater meaning.

[]: Re: Name of Adam's children             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 31, 2006 11:08 pm Whoa. I didn't think InvaderRen's post was that hard to understand. You guys need to read before you post. InvaderRen wrote: if i remember right, the term angel was given to the children of Adam my the Lillim. Shouldnt the Children of Adam have a real name?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Sep 01, 2006 6:42 am Crosswalk told me that it means "man" (like... "man", "human", "mankind", all that man stuff)

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Sep 01, 2006 9:58 am Legend of the Past wrote: but since SEELE had to orgasms from Judeo-Christian motifs they called them Angels, mostly because them being the harbringers, messengers, of apocalypse\Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 01, 2006 12:33 pm Legend of the Past wrote: Almost. Human is 'Ben Adam', meaning 'Son of Man'.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Sep 01, 2006 4:17 pm Reichu wrote: Legend of the Past wrote: Almost. Human is 'Ben Adam', meaning 'Son of Man'.

[]:             Quote Post by Rawinder » Fri Sep 01, 2006 4:28 pm Reichu wrote: Sexist pigs!

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Sep 01, 2006 4:35 pm That's right, because the myths got so mangled together and ideas were borrowed enough to ever seperate them again. Where do you think the feathered wings came from? They sure didn't come from Jewish lore-The looks of an Angel are not described, except for when the Big Guy tells the Angels 'Let us make a man in our image', which leads to the idea that angels 'resemble' God (i.e., the closest thing to a physical incarnation of him) and that men resemble God. No wings, no such stuff. The wings were added later on, by christians, as Jews would never go so far as so bluntly change something in their own bible (The Angel who visited Aberham actually looked like a human being, but was uncapable of eating or drinking). All the Lilith stuff is in fact addendum, and she's not so far as mentioned in the bible (unlike Sammael) but only in Jewish lore. Like I said, this stuff gets mangled and squeezed together, and lots of people probably think Lilith is in fact a christian myth. It pisses me off for that reason-if the myths are so big and important, at least be able to tell one from the other-and if you can't do that, AT LEAST stay consistent!

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Sep 01, 2006 10:16 pm The concept of a winged angel didn't occur until the Roman Empire transformed into the Catholic Church, and they renamed all of their paegan statues to be Christian-themed. Many of the statues that we typically associate with angels were originally of greko/roman gods. The bible is fairly ambiguous as to what an angel looks like, and often times they came disguised as humans.

[]: Re: Name of Adam's children             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sat Sep 02, 2006 11:08 am InvaderRen wrote: if i remember right, the term angel was given to the children of Adam my the Lillim. Shouldnt the Children of Adam have a real name? Im not even sure what language is the source of the Lillith-Lillim relationship, so i cant even take a stab at that... I think that the term Adamite is a valid term. "-ite" means decended from, thus Adamite means the decendants of Adam. Have you ever wondered what the Adamites call us?

I think that the term Adamite is a valid term. "-ite" means decended from, thus Adamite me
